name: Creole Christmas Cake
recipeInstructions:
This very rich cake from the West Indies is ideal for those who prefer not to ice their Christmas cakes. Indeed, icing is not recommended. Just tie a brightly colored ribbon around it and you have a real feast of cake. The fruit and nuts are soaked for a week before baking.
1. Six to eight days before you intend to bake the cake, measure out the rum, brandy, cherry brandy, Cointreau, water, and bitters into a large saucepan.
2. Add the spices, sugar, fruit, and nuts. Stir the ingredients together and heat them over a very low heat until the liquid is just moving. Do not let it boil.
3. Simmer for about 15 minutes. Tip the mixture into a mixing bowl or other glass or china container and allow it to cool completely. Cover the bowl with a double layer of clingfilm and place in the refrigerator or a very cool place.
4. Stir the mixture from time to time during the next few days.
5. Six to eight days later, preheat the oven to Gas 1, 275°F (140°C). Cream the butter and sugar together and fold in the flour and beaten eggs.
6. Add the fruit mixture a little at a time until it is evenly blended.
7. Spoon the cake mixture into the prepared tin, level the top, and bake in the preheated oven for about 3 1/2 to 4 hours.
8. If you think the cake is becoming too brown, place a double thickness of greaseproof paper over the top as protection.
9. When the cake is quite cold, wrap it in greaseproof paper first, then either place it well in a plastic cake box or wrap it in silver foil.
This cake will keep for a few weeks.
